beats.narration: Magnetic particle testing ends at your eyes. So the standards treat light like amperage — measured, recorded, with hard minimums. Here are the numbers.

payload.title: MEASURE THE LIGHT
payload.sub: Light is a measured variable with hard minimums.

beats.narration: Memorize three numbers: 100 footcandles of white light for visible particles. 1,000 microwatts per square centimeter of UV A. Ambient: two footcandles, max.
Verify with calibrated meters at the examination surface — radiometer for UV A, photometer for ambient white light — every shift, after bulb changes, after power interruptions.

payload.heading: Prove it with a calibrated meter
payload.note: SENSOR AT THE SURFACE · RECHECK EVERY SHIFT

beats.narration: A cracked or missing filter leaks harsher UV B — that lamp comes out of service. Never stare into the beam; wear UV blocking spectacles.
Treat light like an instrument. Measure it, log it, protect the eyes — every indication you ever find ends there.

payload.sub: Every indication ends at your eye.
